item.title: NYT Strands #836 Hints and Answers for Today, June 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #836 for Wednesday, June 17, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

Before getting into the answers of today’s puzzle, here’s how you can have the cleanest reading experience for this and all of our stories: download the free Insider Gaming App for iOS or Android. 

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, June 17, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #836 are all words related to farming.

General Strands Hints for June 76

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Item used to build sandcastles or carry water.
  • Hint 2: These are usually seen in the color red and are used for bailing hay.
  • Hint 3: This farming apparatus is also the name of a music magazine.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #836 for June 17, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for June 17, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • HORS
  • HORSE
  • HORSES
  • WORK
  • RATE

Today’s Strands Answer for Game #836 on June 17, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • WHEELBARROW
  • PITCHFORK
  • SAWHORSE
  • TRACTOR
  • BUCKET
  • SPANAGRAM: FARMING

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #836?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on June 18 will be Strands Game #837

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Epic Games Says Unreal Engine 6 Will Deepen AI Integration
item.content:encoded:
Epic Games reveals that Unreal Engine 6 will further integrate AI into the development process. The forthcoming game engine will change a lot about how games are made, according to Epic.

Unreal Engine 6 was teased during a recent Rocket League Championship Series event. It was announced that the game would be entering a new era in Unreal Engine 6 after over a decade on Unreal Engine 3. Now, new details have come to light regarding the future of game development with UE.

Before getting into the meat of the matter, here’s how you can have the cleanest reading experience for this and all of our stories: download the free Insider Gaming App for iOS or Android.  

Unreal Engine 6 Will Reduce Tedious Development Tasks With AI

During the State of Unreal presentation on June 17, Epic revealed how the next era of its engine is shaping up differently from its predecessors. It stated that over the next two years, UE5 and Unreal Editor for Fortnite will be merged to create Unreal Engine 6. They also mentioned that they will be introducing integration with AI models, such as Claude and Gemini, and an effort to reduce the redundancy of game development.

“Our goal for UE6 is to greatly reduce the tedious work in authoring content to leave more time for creative exploration, and increase the amount of iterations a team can make to polish their content. UE6 will ship with tools and workflows where you can choose to bring your own favorite models, battle-tested against internal development and in UEFN,” Epic said (via VGC)

  • More AI News: Sony Exploring Generative AI with Bandai Namco

AI is still the hot-button tech topic. In the world of gaming, it regularly faces backlash from players when it is revealed that a developer has used generative AI and its processes. However, many of the top names in gaming do believe that AI will power the future of development.

How do you feel about this news concerning Epic’s plan to increase AI integration into the development process? Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server.

For more, check out news concerning a free GTA 5 upgrade for last-gen console users. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: ‘Price Increases Are Unavoidable,’ Tim Cook Warns Apple Users
item.content:encoded:
Tim Cook has commented on the unavoidable rise in the cost of Apple products as memory prices surge. Cook currently serves as the chief executive of the tech conglomerate and recently opened up about the tumultuous state of the RAM market.

During a recent interview, he spoke about the unfortunate news of plans to raise product prices, as well as what the company has done to try to prevent these increases in the past.

Before you go any further, here’s how you can have the cleanest reading experience for this and all of our stories: download the free Insider Gaming App for iOS or Android. 

Tim Cook Says Rising Memory Prices Leave Apple Few Options

In an interview with the Wall Street Journal, Tim Cook called the upcoming rise in prices unavoidable. He also noted that they had been doing their best to mitigate these increases.

“Unfortunately, price increases are unavoidable,” he said. “We’re doing our best to mitigate the huge increases that are being passed to us, and we’ve been trying to shield our customers from the increases, but the situation has become unsustainable.”

  • MORE Cost Concern-Related News: XBOX Reevaluating Next-Generation Console Amid Cost Concerns

Apple hasn’t unveiled its next line of iPhones. Those are usually revealed around September at an exclusive Apple event. This year, it’s expected that Apple will finally unveil a folding iPhone. Android brands, especially Samsung, have been producing foldable models for several years now.

New high-end iPhones have been over $1,000 for several years. While it’s not known how high prices will be set, Apple could see its new era, led by new CEO John Ternus, kick off with a struggle if prices are too high even for the most loyal Apple customers. Tim Cook is stepping down as CEO on September 1. However, he will remain with the brand as the executive chairman.

How do you feel about an increase in prices for Apple products? Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server.

For more on the rising cost of memory, check out the latest news regarding PlayStation 6. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
